domain: Aboriginal songline (Dreaming-track oral routes encoding geography in lyric sequences; singing reproduces the route via spatial waypoints; sequential memory anchored to landscape positions; transmits navigation + genealogy + ecology)
specific_mechanism: Ordered-sequential memory anchored to spatial waypoints; retrieved by traversing the sequence rather than random-access keyed lookup.
llm_application: SONGLINE-MEM: an ordered-sequence external memory store for LLM agents. Entries M[0], M[1], ..., M[K-1] are stored as an EXPLICIT SEQUENCE (not key-value). Each entry has a spatial-semantic anchor (e.g., location, page, timestamp). Retrieval API: traverse(start_anchor, direction='forward', step=K) returns next-K sequential entries. Forward + backward traversal. Differs from H-MEM/GAM/A-MEM/Knowledge Capsules (semantic random-access keyed retrieval) by being SEQUENCE-PRESERVING and TRAVERSAL-BASED.
novelty_claim: H-MEM uses positional index for hierarchical retrieval. A-MEM uses entity-centric attribute-value. GAM uses graph-guided multi-factor. ByteRover uses LLM-curated hierarchical context. Knowledge Capsules use structured memory units. All use RANDOM-ACCESS keyed retrieval. SONGLINE-MEM differs by (a) EXPLICIT SEQUENTIAL ORDER as primary structure, (b) TRAVERSAL-based retrieval API, (c) spatial-semantic waypoint anchors, (d) forward + backward navigation.
